LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: CROSS-COMPLAINANT CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES, ON ITS CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH FOUR IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF CCP'S DAMAGES. THE PARTIES DISPUTE THE SCOPE OF CCP'S WORK AND WHETHER IT WAS RETAINED TO WORK ON THE BERKLEY ACTION AND OUGHT TO RECOVER FOR THAT WORK. (SEE PETERSON DECL., PAR. 19 ["CCP AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS AGREED THAT CCP WOULD DEFEND THE BERKLEY ACTION UNDER THE SAME TERMS OF THE FEBRUARY 29, 2016 AGREEMENT.]; LEVINSON DECL., PAR. 107 ["CC&P PROCEEDED TO REPRESENT US IN THE BERKLEY ACTION WITHOUT SEEKING OUR CONSENT TO DO SO AND BILLED US FOR THIS UNAUTHORIZED REPRESENTATION."].) CROSS-DEFENDANTS' ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT CCP REPRESENTED THEM IN THE BERKLEY ACTION, OR THEIR CONTEMPORANEOUS FAILURE TO OBJECT TO CCP'S REPRESENTATION, ARE NOT NECESSARILY CONCLUSIVE AS TO WHETHER THAT REPRESENTATION WAS UNDERTAKEN WITH THEIR CONSENT, NOR DO THEY SQUARELY ADDRESS THE CONTRACTUAL TERMS GOVERNING THAT REPRESENTATION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

Defendant And Cross-Complainant Carlson, Calladine And Peterson Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, Evidentiary Sanctions
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant And Cross-Complainant Carlson, Calladine And Peterson LLP's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, Evidentiary Sanctions.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP's motion for an order granting terminating sanctions or, in the alternative, evidentiary sanctions is granted in part as follows: Plaintiffs may not introduce in evidence at trial any evidence or documents that was not previously disclosed or produced to Defendant in the course of this litigation prior to the hearing on this motion. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 2023.030(c) [court may prohibit a party "from introducing designated matters in evidence"]; see Juarez v. Boy Scouts of America, Inc. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 377, 389; Pate v. Channel Lumber Co. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1447, 1454.) The arguments raised in Plaintiffs' opposition to the motion are meritless.

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Cross-Complainant Carlson, Calladine And Peterson Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Nov-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

CROSS-COMPLAINANT CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues, on its causes of action one through four is DENIED in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the amount of CCP's damages. The parties dispute the scope of CCP's work and whether it was retained to work on the Berkley action and ought to recover for that work. (See Peterson Decl., par. 19 ["CCP and Cross-Defendants agreed that CCP would defend the Berkley Action under the same terms of the February 29, 2016 Agreement.]; Levinson Decl., par. 107 ["CC&P proceeded to represent us in the Berkley action without seeking our consent to do so and billed us for this unauthorized representation."].) Cross-Defendants' allegations in the Second Amended Complaint that CCP represented them in the Berkley action, or their contemporaneous failure to object to CCP's representation, are not necessarily conclusive as to whether that representation was undertaken with their consent, nor do they squarely address the contractual terms governing that representation.

Written order is signed and filed.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
KevinSullivan, Esq., Law Offices of Kevin Sullivan, 415-860-2170, appearing for Plaintiff Masha Levinson et al;
James k. Holder, Esq., Gordon & Rees, 415-986-5900, appearing for Defendant Brian Maschler, et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GORDON & REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP, BRIAN MASCHLER, and RYAN POLK'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendants Gordon & Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP, Brian Maschler and Ryan Polk's demurrer to the second amended complaint is OVERRULED. Defendants rely on CCP 340.6's one-year statute of limitations. But they must establish from the complaint's face and the judicially-noticed materials that the complaint is "necessarily barred." (Favila v. Kattan Muchin Rosenman LLP (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 189, 224) (defendant's burden; "statute of limitations will not lie where the action may be, but is not necessarily barred").)

Defendants' main argument is that plaintiffs learned defendants had not secured return of certain funds more than a year before suing them for malpractice, and thus "discovered" defendants' "wrongful act or omission" outside the limitations period. (See CCP 340.6(a).) Plaintiffs do not seem to dispute the bare fact that they learned the funds were not returning, but plead that defendants misrepresented the reasons why, blaming court error rather than admitting defendants' own shortcomings. (SAC 46-47, 54, 59.) Plaintiffs are lay people; indeed, they filed their initial complaint pro per. Whether plaintiffs discovered defendants' purported wrongful acts or omissions outside the limitations period raises issues more susceptible to summary adjudication than demurrer. (See Krusesky v. Baugh (1982) 138 Cal.App.3d 562, 567-68)(what constitutes malpractice notice "turns on the facts of each case").)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR#8098 email: gcasey@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/RBU)
